case_title: CAROL A. TRUFANT VS. POLICE CHIEF RE: OFFICERS RONALD MCGOLDRICK AND

docket_entries.description: MOTION FOR 31 DAY CONTINUANCE RE: ANTI-SLAPP FILED IN CASE FILED BY PLAINTIFF TRUFANT, CAROL A.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: MATTER DISCUSSED. AT CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSION, THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE HEARING ON DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERGENT CRAIG TOM AND OFFICER RONALD MCGOLDRICK'S MOTION TO DISMISS AND SPECIAL MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT PER CCP 425.16 IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 9, 2013 AT 9:30 AM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.
NOTICE OF MOTION FOR DISCOVERY PURSUANT TO 425.16(G), PROOF OF SERVICE FILED BY PLAINTIFF TRUFANT, CAROL A. HEARING SET FOR AUG-12-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

PROOF OF SERVICE BY FIRST CLASS MAIL FILED BY PLAINTIFF TRUFANT, CAROL A.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERGENT CRAIG TOM AND OFFICER RONALD MCGOLDRICK'S MOTION TO DISMISS AND SPECIAL MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT PER CCP 425.16 IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 9, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 AND WILL BE HEARD ON THE MERITS ON THAT DATE. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CAROL TRUFANT'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DENYING MOTION FOR DISCOVERY IS DENIED BECAUSE NO NEW DEVELOPMENTS OR NEW MATTERS HAVE OCCURED FROM THE LAST HEARING TO CHANGE THE RULING. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302)
PROOF OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY; DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF TRUFANT, CAROL A. SERVED SEP-09-2013, MAIL AS TO DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 2.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERGENT CRAIG TOM & OFFICER RONALD MCGOLDRICK'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE SATISFIED THEIR BURDEN OF MAKING A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT THE ALLEGED WRONGFUL CONDUCT ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY UNDER CCP 425.16. WRITTEN AND ORAL COMMUNICATIONS MADE BY POLICE OFFICERS WITHIN AN OFFICIAL PROCEEDING OF THE LAW FALLS WITHIN THE PURVIEW OF THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE. (SEE SCHAFFER V. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO 168 CAL. APP. 4TH 992, 1003-04 (2008); BRADBURY V. SUPERIOR COURT, 49 CAL. APP. 4TH 1008, 1116 (1996).) THE ALLEGED CONDUCT OF DEFENDANTS SUBMITTING A FALSE INCIDENT REPORT ON FEBRUARY 21, 2013, FALLS WITH THE PURVIEW OF THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE BECAUSE IT IS A WRITTEN REPORT WITHIN AN OFFICIAL PROCEEDING OF THE LAW. PLAINTIFF HAS ALSO FAILED TO ESTABLISH THAT THE UNCONTESTED EVIDENCE CONCLUSIVELY SHOWS THAT DEFENDANTS' CONDUCT WAS ILLEGAL AS A MATTER OF LAW. (SEE CROSS V. COOPER (2011) 197 CAL. APP. 4TH 357, 384.) THE BURDEN THEN SHIFTS TO PLAINTIFF TO DEMONSTRATE A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRODUCED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO DEMONSTRATE A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON HER CLAIMS. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR PREVAILING PARTY ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. A PROPER NOTICED MOTION SHOULD BE FILED. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.*

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CAROL TRUFANT, 510.221.4504, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
ELIABETH PEDERSON, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANTS.

ULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERGENT CRAIG TOM and OFFICER RONALD MCGOLDRICK's Motion For $8,625.00 in attorney's fees and costs is granted pursuant to CCP 425.16(c). The motion is timely filed and reasonable considering the complexity of issues, expertise of counsel, and amount of time involved. (See Russell v. Foglio (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 653, 661.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

ELIZABETH PETERSON, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3862, FOR DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERGENT CRAIG TOM AND OFFICER RONALD MCGOLDRICK.

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK PLAINTIFF CAROL TRUFANT'S Motion For Additional Discovery UNDER SUBMISSION. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED THE COURT WITH A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
*AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CAROL TRUFANT'S Motion For Additional Discovery under CCP 425.16(g) is denied as Plaintiff has not demonstrated good cause. Plaintiff has not shown that defendants possess evidence needed for Plaintiff to establish a prima facie case, or what additional facts Plaintiff intends to uncover. (See 1-800 Contacts, Inc. v. Steinberg, 107 Cal. App. 4th 568, 593 (2003).) Order signed AFTER HEARING.*

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss And Special Motion To Dismiss Pltf'S Complaint Per Ccp 425.16
Off Calendar Oct-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CAROL TRUFANT, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
ELIZABETH PETERSON, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3862, FOR DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERGENT CRAIG TOM AND OFFICER RONALD MCGOLDRICK.

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERGENT CRAIG TOM & OFFICER RONALD MCGOLDRICK'S Special Motion To strike UNDER SUBMISSION. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED THE COURT WITH A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
*AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SERGENT CRAIG TOM & OFFICER RONALD MCGOLDRICK'S Special Motion To strike is granted. Defendants have satisfied their burden of making a prima facie showing that the alleged wrongful conduct arises from protected activity under CCP 425.16. Written and oral communications made by police officers within an official proceeding of the law falls within the purview of the anti-SLAPP statute. (See Schaffer v. City and County of San Francisco 168 Cal. App. 4th 992, 1003-04 (2008); Bradbury v. Superior Court, 49 Cal. App. 4th 1008, 1116 (1996).) The alleged conduct of Defendants submitting a false Incident Report on February 21, 2013, falls with the purview of the anti-SLAPP statute because it is a written report within an official proceeding of the law. Plaintiff has also failed to establish that the uncontested evidence conclusively shows that Defendants' conduct was illegal as a matter of law. (See Cross v. Cooper (2011) 197 Cal. App. 4th 357, 384.) The burden then shifts to Plaintiff to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff has not produced sufficient evidence to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on her claims.
Defendants' request for prevailing party attorney fees is denied without prejudice. A proper noticed motion should be filed. Order signed AFTER HEARING.*

Notice Of Motion For Discovery Pursuant To 425.16(G)
Off Calendar Aug-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CAROL TRUFANT, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CAROL TRUFANT's Motion For Discovery Pursuant To 425.16(G) is Denied. Plaintiff failed to demonstrate good cause for the requested discovery under CCP 425.16(g). Plaintiff's July 22, 2013 "Amended Notice" is improper but in any event fails to establish good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.
